suggestions.name: RenderingStyleRegistry
suggestions.rationale: These members form a cohesive visual styling system that manages paint, stroke, and shape attributes for chart rendering with series-specific overrides and default fallbacks. Extracting them into a registry centralizes all styling concerns and reduces the complexity of the main renderer class while improving reusability across different chart types.
suggestions.reasoning:
Step 1: Primary Responsibility Analysis
These methods collectively manage visual styling attributes for chart rendering, specifically focusing on paints, strokes, and shapes used across different series and contexts (legend text, item labels, fills, outlines). They provide a consistent pattern of lookup/get/set operations with fallback to default values, plus serialization support for all visual attributes.

Step 2: Shared Concept/Domain
All members belong to the "visual styling" domain of chart rendering. They represent a cohesive styling system that manages how chart elements appear visually - colors, line styles, shapes, and text formatting. The pattern of series-specific maps with default fallbacks is consistent across all styling attributes.

Step 3: Extraction Benefits
- Single Responsibility Principle: Isolates all visual styling logic from core rendering logic
- Code Reusability: Styling system can be reused across different renderer types
- Complexity Reduction: Removes dozens of styling-related fields and methods from the main renderer class
- Testability: Visual styling can be tested independently of rendering algorithms
- Maintainability: All styling logic is centralized, making it easier to modify or extend

Step 4: Design Justification
This follows the Extract Class refactoring pattern to separate concerns. The styling attributes form a cohesive subsystem that can be encapsulated as a Strategy pattern component, allowing different styling strategies to be plugged into renderers. This also supports the Decorator pattern for layering different visual effects.

suggestions.name: SeriesPaintRegistry
suggestions.rationale: These methods form a cohesive unit responsible for managing the association between data series and their paint properties. Extracting them into a SeriesPaintRegistry creates a focused component that encapsulates series-to-paint mapping logic, improving separation of concerns and making the paint management functionality reusable across different rendering contexts.
suggestions.reasoning:
Step 1: Primary Responsibility Analysis
These methods collectively manage the mapping between data series indices and their corresponding Paint objects. They provide CRUD operations (Create/Read/Update/Delete) for storing, retrieving, updating, and clearing paint assignments for different data series in a chart rendering context.

Step 2: Shared Concept/Domain
These members represent a specialized mapping abstraction that associates series identifiers (integers) with visual Paint properties. This is essentially a registry or lookup table that maintains the visual styling information for chart series, with notification capabilities when changes occur.

Step 3: Extraction Benefits
- Single Responsibility Principle: Isolates series-to-paint mapping logic from other rendering concerns
- Code reusability: This mapping concept could be reused for other series-to-property associations
- Complexity reduction: Removes paint management complexity from the main renderer class
- Testability: Paint mapping logic can be tested independently of rendering logic

Step 4: Design Justification
This follows the Extract Class refactoring pattern to create a specialized Registry pattern. The extracted class will have high cohesion (all methods deal with series paint mapping) and low coupling (minimal dependencies, only needs change notification capability).

suggestions.name: SeriesStrokeRegistry
suggestions.rationale: These methods form a cohesive registry for managing stroke properties indexed by series number. Extracting them creates a focused component that encapsulates series-to-stroke mapping logic, improving separation of concerns and making the stroke management functionality reusable across different renderer implementations.
suggestions.reasoning:
Step 1: Primary Responsibility Analysis
These methods collectively manage stroke configurations for different data series in a chart renderer. They provide functionality to get, set, and clear stroke properties that are indexed by series number, maintaining a mapping between series identifiers and their visual stroke attributes.

Step 2: Shared Concept/Domain
All members belong to the "series stroke configuration" domain. They represent a cohesive subsystem for managing visual stroke properties (line thickness, dash patterns, etc.) on a per-series basis. The seriesStrokeMap field and all methods operate exclusively on this mapping concept.

Step 3: Extraction Benefits
- Single Responsibility Principle: Isolates stroke management logic from other rendering concerns
- Code reusability: The stroke registry can be reused by different renderer types
- Complexity reduction: Removes series-specific stroke logic from the main renderer class
- Testability: Stroke management can be tested independently of rendering logic
- Encapsulation: Centralizes all stroke-related state and behavior

Step 4: Design Justification
This follows the Extract Class refactoring pattern to create a specialized registry/collection class. It implements the Registry pattern by maintaining a mapping of series identifiers to stroke objects, with proper encapsulation and change notification capabilities.

suggestions.name: SeriesItemLabelPaintRegistry
suggestions.rationale: These methods form a cohesive registry that maps series indices to their corresponding item label Paint objects. Extracting them creates a specialized collection that encapsulates series-based paint configuration with proper change notification, improving separation of concerns from the main rendering logic.
suggestions.reasoning:
Step 1: Primary Responsibility Analysis
These methods collectively manage the storage, retrieval, and modification of Paint objects specifically for item labels on a per-series basis. They provide a complete CRUD interface (get, set, clear) for associating Paint objects with series indices, with optional change notification functionality.

Step 2: Shared Concept/Domain
All members belong to the "series-based item label paint configuration" domain. They encapsulate the concept of a configurable mapping between series identifiers and their corresponding Paint objects used for rendering item labels, forming a cohesive data structure with its associated operations.

Step 3: Extraction Benefits
- Single Responsibility Principle: Isolates series-to-paint mapping logic from the broader rendering concerns
- Code reusability: This mapping pattern could be reused for other series-based configurations
- Complexity reduction: Removes specialized collection management from the main renderer class
- Testability: The mapping logic can be tested independently of rendering functionality

Step 4: Design Justification
This follows the Extract Class refactoring pattern to create a specialized collection wrapper. The extracted class represents a "Registry" pattern, maintaining a mapping between series identifiers and their associated Paint configurations with proper encapsulation and change notification support.

suggestions.name: LegendFontRegistry
suggestions.rationale: These methods form a cohesive unit responsible for managing and retrieving legend text fonts for chart series. They maintain a registry of series-to-font mappings with fallback logic, representing a clear single responsibility that can be extracted to improve the renderer's focus and enable reusability across different chart components.
suggestions.reasoning:
Step 1: Primary Responsibility Analysis
These methods collectively manage legend text fonts for chart series. They provide functionality to get, set, lookup, and clear fonts specifically for legend text display, with support for both series-specific fonts and a default fallback font. The primary responsibility is managing the font configuration for legend text rendering.

Step 2: Shared Concept/Domain
All members belong to the "legend text font management" domain. They work together to maintain a mapping between series and their corresponding legend text fonts, with a coherent API for font retrieval that includes fallback logic. This represents a cohesive subsystem within the larger chart rendering framework.

Step 3: Extraction Benefits
- Single Responsibility Principle: Isolates legend font management from other rendering concerns
- Code reusability: The font management logic could be reused by other chart components that need legend text fonts
- Complexity reduction: Removes font-specific logic from the main renderer class, making it more focused
- Testability: Legend font behavior can be tested in isolation without the complexity of the full renderer

Step 4: Design Justification
This follows the Extract Class refactoring pattern to separate concerns. The extracted class would have high cohesion (all methods deal with legend text fonts) and low coupling (minimal dependencies - only needs to fire change events). This aligns with the Single Responsibility Principle by creating a focused component responsible solely for legend font management.

suggestions.name: PositiveItemLabelPositionRegistry
suggestions.rationale: These methods form a cohesive unit responsible for managing and retrieving positive item label positions through a series-to-position mapping system. Extracting them creates a focused registry that encapsulates the lookup logic and default fallback behavior, reducing the complexity of the main renderer class while improving the organization of label positioning concerns.
suggestions.reasoning:
Step 1: Primary Responsibility Analysis
These methods collectively manage the positioning of positive item labels in chart rendering. They handle getting, setting, and clearing label positions at both the series level and default level, maintaining a mapping between series and their specific label positions.

Step 2: Shared Concept/Domain
All methods operate within the "positive item label positioning" domain. They share two key data structures (positiveItemLabelPositionMap and defaultPositiveItemLabelPosition) and provide a complete API for managing how positive values are labeled and positioned on charts. This represents a cohesive subsystem for label positioning configuration.

Step 3: Extraction Benefits
- Single Responsibility Principle: Isolates label positioning logic from other rendering concerns
- Code reusability: The positioning logic could be reused across different renderer types
- Complexity reduction: Removes 8 methods and 2 fields from the AbstractRenderer god class
- Testability: Label positioning behavior can be tested in isolation without chart rendering complexity

Step 4: Design Justification
This follows the Extract Class refactoring pattern to separate configuration management concerns. The extracted class would act as a specialized registry/configuration object that encapsulates the mapping and lookup logic for item label positions, following the Single Responsibility Principle.
